note: Synthetic consultation snippets with manually curated, immutable gold labels. No real patient data. Products and medications are limited to those present in evidence_records.json. Gold labels reflect clinical reasoning and must not be altered to improve metrics. v0.2.0: two cases relabeled under the realtime task spec (see relabeledInV2 fields) and three cases added for spec-mandated behaviors ('the pill' ambiguity, closed-class herbal inducer, discussion-only mention). Relabels reflect specification semantics changes, never metric tuning.

cases.input: The patient currently uses the combined pill and takes carbamazepine.
cases.rationale: Both a hormonal product and the interacting medication are explicitly current.

cases.input: She is being treated with rifampicin for tuberculosis and uses a combined oral contraceptive.
cases.rationale: Combined hormonal contraceptive with a current potent enzyme inducer (rifampicin).

cases.input: The patient takes amlodipine, ramipril and carbamazepine daily, and uses a combined oral contraceptive.
cases.rationale: Multiple concurrent medications; the interacting one (carbamazepine) must be identified among non-interacting drugs.

cases.input: She has the etonogestrel implant and takes carbamazepine for epilepsy.
cases.rationale: Etonogestrel implant with a current enzyme inducer (carbamazepine).

cases.input: She has requested the morning-after pill and is currently taking rifampicin.
cases.rationale: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception with a current hepatic enzyme inducer.

cases.input: She uses Nexplanon and takes Tegretol.
cases.rationale: Brand names (Nexplanon = etonogestrel implant, Tegretol = carbamazepine) must be normalized to generic identifiers.

cases.input: She takes lamotrigine for epilepsy and has just started a combined oral contraceptive.
cases.rationale: Reversed-direction interaction: the estrogen-containing contraceptive lowers lamotrigine levels.

cases.input: She uses a combined oral contraceptive and takes carbamazapine daily.
cases.rationale: Misspelled 'carbamazapine' must be normalized via documented synonyms to carbamazepine.

cases.input: She uses a combined oral contraceptive and takes paracetamol occasionally.
cases.rationale: Paracetamol is not an interacting medication in the prototype dataset; retrieval runs but finds no record.

cases.input: She takes the progestogen-only pill and uses ibuprofen for headaches.
cases.rationale: Ibuprofen is not an interacting medication in the prototype dataset.

cases.input: He takes sertraline and uses a combined oral contraceptive.
cases.rationale: Sertraline (an SSRI) is not an interacting medication in the prototype dataset.

cases.input: She uses a combined oral contraceptive but is not taking carbamazepine.
cases.rationale: The interacting medication is explicitly negated and excluded from retrieval.

cases.input: She has the etonogestrel implant and denies any use of rifampicin or other enzyme inducers.
cases.rationale: Explicit denial of enzyme-inducer use; excluded from retrieval.

cases.input: She uses the combined pill. Her partner takes carbamazepine.
cases.rationale: Another person's medication is excluded context (spec §17.4); the patient has no interacting medication.
cases.relabeledInV2: v1 expected NO_VALIDATED_MATCH; spec §17.4 assigns other-person exclusions to EXCLUDED_CONTEXT.

cases.input: She stopped carbamazepine last year and currently uses a combined oral contraceptive.
cases.rationale: The interacting medication was stopped in the past and is excluded from retrieval.

cases.input: She used rifampicin during treatment two years ago; she now uses the progestogen-only pill.
cases.rationale: Rifampicin use was historical (two years ago) and is excluded from retrieval.

cases.input: She takes carbamazepine and says she uses contraception, but the method is unclear.
cases.rationale: The hormonal method is not identifiable; retrieval is withheld pending clarification.

cases.input: She uses a combined oral contraceptive and is planning to start carbamazepine next month.
cases.rationale: A planned enzyme inducer with a current combined hormonal contraceptive is an eligible proposed combination (spec §15.4, §16.5); the warning is labelled proposed-context.
cases.relabeledInV2: v1 expected MORE_INFORMATION_REQUIRED; spec §15.4/§16.5 make planned medications eligible as proposed pairs.

cases.input: She takes carbamazepine and might be using some form of hormonal contraception.
cases.rationale: Hormonal contraceptive use is uncertain and the method unspecified; retrieval is withheld.

cases.input: She uses a combined oral contraceptive and takes something for her epilepsy but cannot recall the name.
cases.rationale: The specific medication is unknown; it cannot be normalized or looked up, so clarification is required.

cases.input: She takes carbamazepine and uses the pill.
cases.rationale: Bare 'the pill' must not be guessed as a specific method (spec §15.7); the system abstains and asks for the exact pill type.

cases.input: She has requested the morning-after pill and takes St John's wort daily.
cases.rationale: A herbal CYP3A4 inducer (St John's wort) is an explicit closed-class member of the levonorgestrel EC record.

cases.input: The doctor explained what carbamazepine is. She uses the combined pill.
cases.rationale: A medication that is merely discussed is excluded context (spec §15.6, §17.4); it never becomes a patient assertion and no warning is produced.

cases.input: She takes carbamazepine and is considering starting the combined pill.
cases.rationale: A hormonal product under consideration with a current enzyme inducer is an eligible proposed combination (spec §15.4 spirit); the warning is labeled proposed-context.
